revision.comment: Bot: mark 1 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24271391609|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:百目鬼]]
'''Dōmeki''' (百目鬼) is an {{ill|Oni|en|Oni|qid=Q730052|lt=oni|1=Oni}} (demon) featured in the legends of {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|qid=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, and is said to be the origin of the place name "Dōmeki."<ref name="minwa">{{Cite book|ja|author=宇都宮市教育委員会|title=宇都宮の民話|year=1983|publisher=宇都宮市教育委員会|page=47|qq=}}</ref><ref name="murakami">{{Cite book|ja|author=村上健司|authorlink=村上健司|title=妖怪事典|year=2005|publisher=毎日新聞社|isbn=4-620-31428-5|page=241|qq=}}</ref>
== Overview == In the {{ill|Hanawada|ja|塙田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanawada|1=Hanawada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11428386}} area of Utsunomiya, there is a place called "Dōmeki," where legends related to this oni are preserved. Several different versions of the story exist, but all feature the oni prominently.<ref name="minwa" /> The name "Dōmeki" remains in use today, such as in the name of the alley "Dōmeki-dōri" (百目鬼通り).<ref name="murakami" /> === The Dōmeki of Usagida === While passing through {{ill|Ōso|ja|大曽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōso|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōso, Utsunomiya), {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Hidesato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1136623}} encountered a mysterious old man who told him to go to Usagida, a horse disposal site<ref>{{Cite web|ja|author=世界大百科事典|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%A6%AC%E6%8D%A8%E5%A0%B4-1274709 |title=馬捨場 |website=コトバンク |publisher=朝日新聞社/VOYAGE GROUP |accessdate=2020-02-03|qq=}}</ref> northwest of Ōso Village. Upon arriving, Hidesato encountered an oni the size of ten {{ill|jō|ja|丈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jō|1=jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4389960}} (approximately 30 meters), with blade-like hair and a hundred {{ill|Eye|en|Eye|qid=Q7364|lt=eyes|1=Eye}}. Hidesato shot it with his {{ill|bow|ja|弓 (武器)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bow|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Bow (weapon)|qid=Q46311}} and defeated it. The wounded Dōmeki fled to {{ill|Mount Myōjin (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Myōjin (Utsunomiya)|1=Mount Myōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11512575}} but continued to emit poisonous vapors and {{ill|Flame|en|Flame|qid=Q235544|lt=flames|1=Flame}} until it was pacified and made to {{ill|Buddhahood|en|Buddhahood|qid=Q56247062|lt=attain enlightenment|1=Buddhahood}} by the spiritual power of a monk named Chitoku from {{ill|Hongan-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|本願寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongan-ji (Utsunomiya)|1=Hongan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11521048}} (originally in Hanawada, later relocated to {{ill|Tsuruta, Aomori|en|Tsuruta, Aomori|qid=Q2358964|lt=Tsuruta, Utsunomiya|1=Tsuruta, Aomori}}).<ref name="murakami" /> === The Dōmeki of the Hundred Caves === In the {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakkuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakkuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}} ("Hundred Caves of Nagaoka"), there once lived an oni who commanded a hundred other oni. Longing to escape the life of a demon, he devoted himself to Buddhism at Hongan-ji (Utsunomiya) and was eventually reborn as a {{ill|Person|en|Person|qid=Q215627|lt=human|1=Person}}. The name "Dōmeki" ("Hundred-Eyed Demon") is said to originate from his position as the {{ill|chieftain|ja|頭目|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chieftain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 首領|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 首領|1=Q121553711|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=DELETED_QID}} of the hundred oni.<ref name="minwa" /><ref name="kyodoshi">{{Cite book|ja|author=徳田浩淳|title=宇都宮郷土史|publisher=文化新報社|year=1959|pages=81, 270|qq=}}</ref> As a token of gratitude, the human-reborn Dōmeki left behind his thumb {{ill|Nail (anatomy)|en|Nail (anatomy)|qid=Q36864|lt=nail|1=Nail (anatomy)}} and a {{ill|crystal|ja|水晶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/crystal|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石英|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石英|1=Quartz|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q43010}} {{ill|Japamala|en|Japamala|qid=Q1336519|lt=juzu (Buddhist prayer beads)|1=Japamala}} at Hongan-ji, where they are said to be preserved.<ref name="kyodoshi" /> == Local Folk Toys == Masks of oni, known as "kanpyō masks" ({{ill|Kanpyō (food)|en|Kanpyō (food)|qid=Q3275606|lt=kanpyō|1=Kanpyō (food)}} masks), made from the fruit of the {{ill|yuugao|ja|ユウガオ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yuugao|1=Lagenaria siceraria var. hispida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21711036}} (bottle gourd), are produced as {{ill|local folk toys|ja|郷土玩具|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/local folk toys|1=Japanese folk toy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11643409}}. These masks are said to be inspired by the legend of Dōmeki.<ref name="murakami" /><ref>{{Cite book|ja|author=西沢笛畝|title=日本郷土玩具事典|publisher=岩崎美術社|year=1965|page=47|qq=}}</ref> == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.bando-bushi.com/legends-of-hidesato Legends of Hidesato - Bandō Warrior Encyclopedia]
{{translated page|ja|百目鬼|version=103869091|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:百目鬼]]
<!--Dōmeki--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q60995805}} [[Category:Oni]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yokai of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with deleted QID in ill template]]
